reasoning: Over a one-week horizon (specifically 4 trading days, assuming the market is closed April 3 for Good Friday), stock movements are largely a random walk. However, Salesforce has recently initiated a massive $25B accelerated share repurchase (ASR) program and board members stepped in to buy shares following a Q4 EPS beat. This provides a strong technical bid and fundamental support underlying the stock, favoring a bounce. While the 2026 YTD trend has been heavily negative (-30%), the short time horizon and active buyback program slightly tilt the odds in favor of an upward move. Therefore, I am forecasting slightly above standard base rates at 54%.
